commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For the moth shall eat them up like a garment, and the worm shall eat them like wool: but my righteousness shall be forever, and my salvation from generation to generation. Cyril of Alexandria The Son is called “salvation” and “righteousness,” as we often say, and thus his quality is declared that he has in himself, by which and in which he is Son by nature. Indeed, he is eternal and does not “wear out. ” Such is the difference between creator and creation, the made from the maker, which is clearly seen in this verse.
